name: Patti Bown
abstract: Patti Bown (July 26, 1931, Seattle, Washington – March 21, 2008, Media, Pennsylvania) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and singer.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Quincy Jones
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of George Russell
New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Savoy Ballroom "The Home of Happy Feet"

works.t: When the Lights Go On Again (All Over the World)

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: All-Star Jam Session

works.t: The New Moon: Softly, as in a Morning Sunrise

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: The rhythm section was Patricia – the girl piano player... Patti Bown... I joined Quincy’s band... The rhythm section was Patricia... Patti Bown was the piano player
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I know. Patti Bown, we were trying to get when she was 12 years old... We wanted to play in nightclubs. They didn’t want us to corrupt her... Patti always wanted to do it, but she had a nervous breakdown... We lost her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Ron Carter was the very first, when we had Ernie Wilkins and Ron Carter. Ron was the beginning, and of course Patti Bown was the original piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
